title: Transaction
description: The money-movement ledger root record. One row per business-level money movement. Carries `tenant_id` only — cascades application/region/organization through the tenant. IMMUTABLE after status='posted'. NEVER carries `application_id`, `region_id`, `organization_id`, `branch_id`, or `scope_node_id`. Reversal via NEW offsetting transaction (source_transaction_id + reversal_reason).

transaction_number.description: Sequential number per (tenant, year). Format 'TXN-YYYY-NNNNNNNN'. Composite unique with tenant_id + YEAR(effective_date). Generated via Postgres advisory lock in TransactionNumberGenerator.

kind.description: One of 12 TransactionKind values. See data/kind-catalog.json.

reference_type.description: Polymorphic reference source class name (Invoice / Payment / Refund / Chargeback / CouponRedemption / TaxRemittance / TenantProvisioning). Null for arbitrary manual adjustments.

reference_id.description: Polymorphic reference source ID. Nullable together with reference_type.

amount_cents.description: Transaction amount in cents. STRICTLY POSITIVE — direction indicated by ledger entries, never by sign. Reversal transactions have their own positive amount + point at the source via source_transaction_id.

currency.description: ISO 4217 3-letter code. Frozen at commit. Every ledger entry in the transaction uses the same currency (single-currency-per-transaction invariant — except for currency_conversion kind which is the one exception).

base_currency.description: ISO 4217 3-letter code. Set only when currency != tenant.base_currency (cross-currency transactions). Equals the tenant's base_currency at commit time.

base_amount_cents.description: Amount in the tenant's base_currency. Set only when currency != tenant.base_currency. Equals ROUND(amount_cents * exchange_rate_to_base) with ±1 cent rounding tolerance.

exchange_rate_to_base.description: The exchange rate captured at commit time. FROZEN — historical reports use this rate, never the current market rate. IAS 21 compliance.

status.description: Enum: draft / posted. Transitions ONE WAY (draft → posted). Posted is IMMUTABLE.

effective_date.description: The settlement date. May differ from created_at for backfilled transactions. Regulator-critical — every GL report keys off effective_date.

posted_at.description: When status transitioned to 'posted'. Set inside the creating hook's atomic transaction. Null while status='draft'.

source_transaction_id.description: Self-referential FK. Set for reversal transactions (points at the transaction being reversed). RESTRICT — the original cannot be hard-deleted while any reversal references it.

reversal_reason.description: Enum: refund / chargeback / error_correction / accounting_adjustment. Travels with source_transaction_id (both set or both null).

description.description: Human-readable ledger-line description. Rendered on GL reports.

metadata.description: Bounded to ≤ 8KB. Support-context that references PII by ID (never PAN / CVV / raw card data). May contain the payment_intent_id (a token, never card data) or the invoice.number reference for cross-check.

created_by_user_id.description: The actor who invoked the recording flow. Null for system-recorded transactions (e.g. RecordInvoicePaidTransactionJob has no human actor).

updated_by.note: Populated only by the observer during the creating→posted transition; refuses to update after posted.

indexes.name: transactions_tenant_number_year_unique
indexes.predicate: extract(year from effective_date) = extract(year from effective_date)
indexes.description: Composite unique index on (tenant_id, transaction_number, YEAR(effective_date)). Enforced by DB — TransactionNumberGenerator uses a Postgres advisory lock keyed on (tenant_id, year) for monotonic generation.

x-invariants: amount_cents > 0 (strictly positive — direction indicated by ledger entries, never by sign)
currency, exchange_rate_to_base, base_amount_cents, base_currency are frozen at post; never mutable after
state transitions are ONE WAY: draft → posted. No draft-from-posted transitions.
posted transactions are IMMUTABLE — TransactionObserver refuses ALL updates + deletes
cross-currency transactions require base_currency + base_amount_cents + exchange_rate_to_base ALL set together (or ALL null for same-currency)
reversal_reason IS NOT NULL iff source_transaction_id IS NOT NULL — they travel as a pair
source_transaction_id must reference a transaction in the same tenant AND in status='posted'
the sum of all associated ledger entries SUM(debit_cents) == SUM(credit_cents) > 0 (enforced at post time; reasserted nightly)
reference_type and reference_id are BOTH set or BOTH null
kind='opening_balance' can occur at most once per tenant
